zh.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Burgess Edward de Watson於大約1240年出生時，他的父親Alexander De Watson31歲，母親Rebecca Dobson31歲。他於1275年與Margaret Irene Ferguson在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1305年在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾享年67歲。

zh-hans.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Burgess Edward de Watson于大约1240年出生时，他的父亲Alexander De Watson31岁，母亲Rebecca Dobson31岁。他于1275年与Margaret Irene Ferguson在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1305年在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Watson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: patronymic meaning ‘son of Wat’, a pet form of Walter that was particularly common in Scotland and northern England. See Watt .

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Burgess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a status name from the Old French word burgeis ‘freeman of a borough’ (a derivative of burg ‘town’, of Germanic origin).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When John Burgess Edward de Watson was born about 1240, his father, Alexander De Watson, was 31 and his mother, Rebecca Dobson, was 31. He married Margaret Irene Ferguson in 1275, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1305, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Burgess Edward de Watson est né environ 1240, son père, Alexander De Watson, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Rebecca Dobson, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Irene Ferguson en 1275, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1305, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Burgess Edward de Watson um 1240 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alexander De Watson 31 und seine Mutter Rebecca Dobson 31. Er hat 1275 in Scotland Margaret Irene Ferguson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1305 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Burgess Edward de Watson è nato il circa 1240, il suo padre, Alexander De Watson, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Rebecca Dobson, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Irene Ferguson nel 1275, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1305, nel Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 67 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Burgess Edward de Watson(이)가 대략 1240년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander De Watson(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Rebecca Dobson(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1275년 스컷랜드에서 Margaret Irene Ferguson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1305년 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Burgess Edward de Watson nasceu aproximadamente 1240, seu pai, Alexander De Watson, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Rebecca Dobson, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Irene Ferguson em 1275, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1305, em Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Burgess Edward de Watson родился около 1240 г., его отцу по имени Alexander De Watson было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Rebecca Dobson, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Irene Ferguson в 1275 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1305 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 67 лет.
